description: Evaluates log management maturity—service governance, KPIs/KRIs, logging systems, unified enterprise log collection, anomaly detection, scope, records, protection, analyst hierarchy, and AI-assisted log analysis.

capabilities.name: Service exists and is in good condition
capabilities.description: A log management service exists, is scoped appropriately, and is reviewed on a regular cadence for efficacy.
evaluation_focus: Enterprise log management service exists
Regular log management service efficacy review

capabilities.name: KPIs/KRIs
capabilities.description: Log management KPIs and KRIs—including log ingestion coverage, pipeline latency, and storage utilization—are documented and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Documented KPIs and KRIs for log management
Regular KPI/KRI review
Log ingestion coverage and pipeline latency metrics
Storage utilization and retention compliance

capabilities.name: Log management system
capabilities.description: A logging system manages and correlates logs enterprise-wide with automation, quarterly configuration review, and integrations.
evaluation_focus: Logging system to manage and correlate all logs
Raw log consumption from all enterprise segments
Automation to process large volumes of logs
Regular logging system configuration review
Integration with compliance, vulnerability, and asset management

capabilities.name: Unified enterprise log collection
capabilities.description: Identity, endpoint, network, application, and data asset technologies send access and activity logs automatically to centralized logging.
evaluation_focus: Automatic centralized identity and access logging
Automatic centralized endpoint device and access logging
Automatic centralized network and access logging
Automatic centralized application and access logging
Automatic centralized data and access logging

capabilities.name: Anomaly detection
capabilities.description: The logging solution monitors and identifies anomalies with configurable automated responses.
evaluation_focus: Anomaly monitoring and identification capability
Configurable automated anomaly responses

capabilities.name: Logging scope
capabilities.description: A clear logging scope is defined and reevaluated at least quarterly to maintain efficacy and storage stability.
evaluation_focus: Clear defined logging scope for devices and technology
Regular scope reevaluation for efficacy and storage stability

capabilities.name: Log records
capabilities.description: The logging solution produces audit logs for all technology that sends logs to it.
evaluation_focus: Audit logs for all log-producing technology

capabilities.name: Log protection
capabilities.description: The log management system stores logs securely and prevents unauthorized changes.
evaluation_focus: Secure log storage and prevention of unauthorized log changes

capabilities.name: Log management team
capabilities.description: A clear hierarchy of T1, T2, and T3 analysts processes logs.
evaluation_focus: T1, T2, and T3 analyst hierarchy for log processing

capabilities.name: AI-assisted log analysis
capabilities.description: AI assists log summarization, anomaly explanation, and investigation query generation with analyst oversight.
evaluation_focus: AI-assisted log summarization and pattern detection
AI-generated investigation queries with analyst review
Governance for AI use in log analysis workflows

evaluation.resiliency_weight_policy: Ask stakeholder for per-capability weights when adding a new evaluation service. This service uses weight 1 for all capabilities.
evaluation.pillar_policy: Some services may have no Technology capabilities; in those cases, do not compute or report a Technology pillar score.
